svg.font-family: -apple-system, SF Pro Display, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Bootstrap · Turn loop · Thinking · Tools · Sub-agents · Resume · Opus strategies

g.text: CLI side — before first API call
Resolve hooks · env · permission policies · settings.json
instructions + tools + CLAUDE.md + skills index (~10–30k tok)
Each loop iteration = one assistant message; loop ends when no tool_use.
Transcript (system + all turns) is re-sent every iteration; cache keeps prefix cheap.
own system prompt · own tool set · own memory
may spawn its own sub-agents (tree of work)
intermediate tokens never pollute parent context
• protect main context from large search output
• run N independent queries in parallel (fan-out)
• route to cheaper/faster model (Haiku) for narrow work
• specialized system prompt per role (reviewer, planner)
First new API call = cache MISS (re-prime server cache)
subsequent turns HIT again · same cold-start penalty as Turn 1
✗ server-side KV cache (TTL ~5min) → refilled on resume
larger reasoning budget · plan before acting
think → tool → think → tool (not batch then act)
emit multiple tool_use blocks in one response
isolate heavy search · route cheap work to Haiku
explicit plan before edits · persist facts to MEMORY.md
⑦ LATENCY PROFILE — why the FIRST message feels slow
compute-bound · scales linearly with prompt size
→ prompt caching collapses prefill; bootstrap is already done
First-turn slowness = (CLI bootstrap) + (cold KV-cache prefill over the full system prompt) + (first-token decode).
Turn 2+ keeps the server KV cache warm, so only the new user message and output tokens pay full cost — feels 5–10× snappier.
Resume restarts this cycle (cache TTL ≈ 5 min server-side), which is why the first post-resume message also feels cold.
